message:
feat(runtime-math): add deterministic JSONL structured logging with certificate load-state tracking

Implement export_runtime_math_log_jsonl() for the runtime-math kernel governance
layer, producing per-decision structured log rows with full traceability fields:

- Add policy certificate load-state tracking via AtomicU8 (none/loaded/verify_failed)
- Emit runtime_decision, runtime_calibration, runtime_snapshot, runtime_certificate_loaded,
  runtime_certificate_verification_failed, runtime_regret_alert, and runtime_drift_alert
  event types with mandatory trace_id, bead_id, scenario_id, decision_path, healing_action,
  errno, latency_ns, and artifact_refs fields
- Add helper mappers: action_name, decision_name, level_for_action, decision_path,
  healing_action_json, family_name, mode_name, sanitize_trace_component, now_utc_iso_like
- Add two unit tests validating required field schema and certificate load outcomes
- Update beads issue tracker (bd-5vr.8 now in_progress with progress notes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-5vr.5","title":"Math: Control/optimization kernels - bandit, primal-dual, mean-field, Schrodinger bridge, rough-path","description":"Implement control/optimization kernels: bandit.rs (constrained MAB Fast/Full routing with regret bounds), control.rs (primal-dual threshold tuning), mean_field_game.rs (Lasry-Lions Nash equilibrium), schrodinger_bridge.rs (entropic OT between workload regimes, Cuturi 2013), rough_path.rs (Lyons rough-path signatures for syscall/memory traces).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.494190448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:25.464193086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["control","frankenlibc","math","optimization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

### 2026-02-14 Progress (PeachCedar)
Implemented a focused logging slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`:
- added policy certificate load-state tracking (`none|loaded|verify_failed`) in `RuntimeMathKernel`
- extended `load_policy_table` to persist successful and failed verification state
- added `export_runtime_math_log_jsonl(mode, bead_id, run_id)` emitting deterministic JSONL rows for:
  - `runtime_decision` (trace/info/warn/error mapped from action, includes `trace_id` + `decision_id` + `decision_action` + `risk_inputs`)
  - `runtime_calibration` (debug)
  - `runtime_snapshot` (info)
  - `runtime_certificate_loaded` / `runtime_certificate_verification_failed`
  - `runtime_regret_alert` and `runtime_drift_alert`
- added helper mappers (`action_name`, `decision_name`, `family_name`, `mode_name`) and deterministic trace/timestamp helpers
- added unit tests:
  - `runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields`
  - `runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_reports_certificate_load_outcomes`

### Verification Commands and Outcomes
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅ pass
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅ pass (existing workspace warnings outside changed file)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ fails on pre-existing clippy violations in untouched files:
  - `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` (`clippy::collapsible-if`)
  - `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/pressure_sensor.rs` (`clippy::manual-range-contains`)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export -- --nocapture` ✅ pass (2 tests)
- `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` ❌ fails on pre-existing tests in untouched file:
  - `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_mutex_core_test.rs`
  - failures: `futex_mutex_contention_increments_wait_and_wake_counters`, `futex_mutex_unlock_without_lock_is_eperm`

### Artifacts / References
- code diff: `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`
- new test names listed above (same file)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PeachCedar","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.789403918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T21:52:48.852539329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","logging","math","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.8","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":338,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.8","author":"PeachCedar","text":"Second-pass schema tightening completed in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.

Note: rch remote sync intermittently fell back to local due worker disk-space rsync failures (No space left on device), but all commands were still initiated via rch as required.","created_at":"2026-02-14T21:48:07Z"},{"id":339,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.8","author":"PeachCedar","text":"Third-pass logging hardening completed.

Delta in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs:
- Added core traceability fields to ALL runtime-math JSONL event types (not only runtime_decision):
  - bead_id, scenario_id, api_family, symbol, decision_path, healing_action, errno, latency_ns
- Applied this schema to:
  - runtime_calibration

  - runtime_regret_alert
  - runtime_drift_alert
- Expanded unit assertions so every exported line is validated for the common schema contract before event-specific checks.

 {"id":"bd-66s","title":"Stub wave 2: getaddrinfo/getnameinfo numeric+files backend","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement numeric host/service resolution and /etc/hosts + /etc/services files backend.
- Deterministic error mapping for unsupported address families/protocols.

Acceptance:
- netdb fixtures pass for numeric and files-backed paths.
- Support matrix marks partial scope explicitly (DNS network backend tracked separately).

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.921326511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","resolver","stubs"],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-66s","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Core resolver module: crates/glibc-rs-core/src/resolv/mod.rs
- AddrInfo struct, EAI_* error codes, AF_* constants
- /etc/hosts parsing: parse_hosts_line, lookup_hosts, reverse_lookup_hosts
- /etc/services parsing: parse_services_line, lookup_service
- getaddrinfo(): numeric IPv4/IPv6 resolution, wildcard fallback
- getnameinfo(): sockaddr_in/sockaddr_in6 to string conversion
- eq_ignore_ascii_case helper
- 35 unit tests covering all functions

### 2. ABI layer: crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs (pre-existing, 519 lines)
- getaddrinfo: numeric IPv4/IPv6 parsing, port parsing, membrane validation
- freeaddrinfo: linked list cleanup
- getnameinfo: numeric address-to-string conversion
- gai_strerror: full EAI_* error mapping

### 3. Status updated: support_matrix.json
- getaddrinfo, getnameinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror: Stub -> Implemented
- Total: Implemented 87->91, Stub 6->2

### 4. Cascading spec updates
- packaging_spec.json: symbol_distribution, Stub_remaining, blockers
- replacement_levels.json: assessment counts, stub_breakdown
- stub_priority_ranking.json: removed resolv_abi from module/symbol rankings, updated burn_down

### Test commands
```
cargo test -p glibc-rs-core --lib resolv  # 35 pass
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness            # 213+ pass, 0 failures
```

All quality gates pass: clippy clean, fmt clean, full harness green.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-66wz","title":"bd-25n subtask: Allocator+membrane invariant/property/adversarial test expansion","description":"Background:
- Allocator/membrane safety claims require broad invariant and adversarial coverage.

Goal:
- Expand allocator/membrane test packs to cover temporal safety, bounds, cache invalidation, and healing behavior comprehensively.

Deliverables:
1) Unit tests for arena generation/quarantine edge cases.
2) Property tests for pointer validation monotonicity and state transitions.
3) Adversarial tests for double-free, foreign-free, UAF, overflow markers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- High-risk allocator/membrane invariants are directly tested and documented.
- Failures report invariant name and violating trace context.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit/property suites with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs with invariant_id, case_id, mode, outcome, timing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.887565117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:54.869522519Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:54.869495258Z","close_reason":"Allocator+membrane invariant/adversarial/property test expansion complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","critique","testing","unit","verification"],"comments":[{"id":124,"issue_id":"bd-66wz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Decomposed bd-66wz into explicit sub-beads to keep the plan self-contained and implementation mechanically checkable:

- bd-66wz.1: Arena quarantine drain invariants + regression tests (QUARANTINE_MAX_BYTES)
- bd-66wz.2: TLS validation cache epoch invalidation regression tests
- bd-66wz.3: Pointer validation adversarial pipeline tests (free/UAF/canary/foreign)
- bd-66wz.4: Deterministic property/invariant tests for allocator+membrane state transitions

Execution intention (can be parallelized if needed): start with bd-66wz.2 + bd-66wz.3 (fast correctness wins), then bd-66wz.1 (quarantine drain edge), then bd-66wz.4 (sequence pressure).

These are designed to directly satisfy the allocator/membrane safety claims in AGENTS.md: quarantine-temporal safety + cache invalidation + canary/fingerprint detection must be covered by deterministic tests with actionable failure context.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:58:38Z"},{"id":129,"issue_id":"bd-66wz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed allocator+membrane test expansion via sub-beads:

- bd-66wz.1 (closed): quarantine drain invariants/regression (arena)
- bd-66wz.2 (closed): TLS cache global epoch invalidation tests + deterministic test lock
- bd-66wz.3 (closed): ptr_validator adversarial tests (foreign free, double-free, canary corruption, cache safety)
- bd-66wz.4 (closed): deterministic sequence/invariant integration test (alloc/free/validate/double-free)

Verification
- `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane -- --nocapture` (unit + integration tests pass).

Impact
- Strengthens TSM allocator claims (quarantine/temporal safety + cache invalidation + canary detection) and unblocks bd-25n critical-path coverage work.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-66wz.1","title":"bd-66wz subtask: Arena quarantine drain invariants + regression tests","description":"Background
- The membrane arena uses a quarantine queue to detect UAF while bounding memory retention.
- If quarantine drain semantics are wrong, we either: (a) lose UAF detection prematurely, (b) leak/quasi-leak freed metadata, or (c) fail under adversarial free storms.

Scope
- Add targeted regression tests in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/arena.rs` for quarantine drain behavior and metadata lifecycle invariants.
- Tests must be deterministic, fast, and failure messages must identify the violated invariant and the sequence that triggered it.

Key Invariants (must be asserted explicitly)
1) Quarantine max-bytes bound: when total quarantined bytes would exceed `QUARANTINE_MAX_BYTES`, the arena deterministically drains/evicts the oldest entries until within budget.
2) Generation safety: drained/evicted entries must not re-validate as live allocations.
3) Metadata hygiene: after drain, allocation headers/canary bookkeeping must not allow stale pointers to validate as Writable/Valid.
4) Idempotence: repeated drain triggers under the same sequence cannot oscillate internal counters.

Test Plan
- Construct a deterministic sequence of alloc/free operations that exceeds the quarantine byte budget (use a small number of large allocations to cross the threshold quickly).
- Assert: drain occurs; arena lookup for freed pointers yields a restrictive state (Freed/Invalid/Quarantined as appropriate) and never Valid/Writable.
- Assert: quarantine accounting counters are non-negative and monotone w.r.t. operations.

Acceptance
- New unit tests in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/arena.rs` cover the above invariants and pass under `cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane`.
- Tests complete quickly (<1s locally) and are deterministic (no time dependence, no RNG without fixed seed).
- Failure output includes: invariant_id, step index, alloc size, and pointer class (live/freed/foreign).

Notes
- This bead is a pure test+invariant closure; do not change arena behavior unless a test demonstrates a real bug.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverLake","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:56:37.356684499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:36.288525141Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:36.288499673Z","close_reason":"Added quarantine drain invariants/regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","membrane","testing","unit","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66wz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-66wz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:56:37.356684499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"bd-66wz.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented deterministic quarantine drain invariant coverage.

Changes
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/arena.rs`:
  - Added `quarantine_drain_evicts_oldest_until_within_budget` that constructs two large quarantine entries in a single shard, forces a drain, and asserts:
    - FIFO eviction order (oldest drained first)
    - quarantine_bytes within `QUARANTINE_MAX_BYTES` after drain
    - addr_to_slot cleanup + slot state transitions to `SafetyState::Freed`
    - free_list receives drained slot index
  - Explicitly deallocates the remaining quarantined entry to avoid retaining ~tens of MB across the test binary lifetime.

Verification
- `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane -- --nocapture` (passes).","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:10:36Z"}]}
